questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Aqueous ammonia is used as a precipitating reagent for Al<sup>3</sup><sup>+</sup> ions as Al(OH)<sub>3</sub> rather than aqueous NaOH, because
questions.solution:
Al(OH)3 formed with NaOH dissolves in excess of NaOH to form aluminate ion.
which is stable.

AlCl 3 + 3 NaOH ⟶ 2 Al OH 3 ↓ + 3 NaCl

Al OH 3 + OH − → Al OH 4 −                                                   Soluble
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following  statements is false ?
options: LiAlH<sub>4</sub> is versatile reducing agent in organic synthesis.
B is always covalent in its compounds
In aqueous solution, the Tl<sup>+</sup> ion is much more stable than Tl (III)
questions.solution:
(i) LiAlH4 is a versatile reducing agent
(ii) NO+ (14 electrons), O2(16 electrons) not isoelectronic
(iii) ​Tl+ is more stable due to Inert pair effect
(iv) B compounds are ionic as well 

B 2 O 3 + 2 NaOH → 2 NaBO 2 + H 2 O  
                                 (Sodium meta Borate)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which is true for an element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> present in the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>13</mn><mi>th</mi></msup></math> group of the periodic table?
options: It is a gas at room temperature.
It forms an oxide of formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>.
It forms a chloride of formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XCl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>.
questions.solution:
The elements of group-13 of the periodic table are solids (examples: B, Al, etc.) at room temperature.

They have a common oxidation state of +3 (as they have three valence electrons in the outermost shell).
So, they form an oxide of formula X2O3 from the ions X3+ and O2-. 

It forms a metal chloride of formula XCl3 and dimer X2Cl6 as the octet state, which accounts for stability is not fulfilled for the metal in the monomer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math> (II) compounds are powerful reducing agents whereas <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></math> (IV) compounds are strong oxidants. It is due to -
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></math> is more electropositive than <math><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math>
Ionization potential of Lead is less than that of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math>

Ionic radii of <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> are larger than those of <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math>
More pronounced inert pair effect in lead than in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math>
questions.solution: Due to inert pair effect, the +2 oxidation state stability increases down the 14th group, and +4 oxidation state stability decreases down the group. Ge (II) tends to acquire Ge (IV) state by loss of electrons. Hence, it acts as a reducing agent. Pb (IV) tends to acquire Pb (II) oxidation state by gain of electrons. Hence, it acts as an oxidising agent.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is correct for the structure of diborane?
options: It contains four <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>e</mi><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo></math>covalent bonds and two <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>e</mi><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo></math>covalent bonds.

It contains three <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>e</mi><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo></math>covalent bonds and three <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>e</mi><mo>)</mo></math> covalent bonds.

It contains two<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>e</mi><mo>)</mo></math> covalent bonds and four <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>e</mi><mo>)</mo></math> covalent bond.
questions.solution: Normal single bond called 2C-2e bond and bridge bond of H called 3C-2e bond.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
What are the correct statements about the elements of group 13 given below?
(I) The stability of +1 oxidation state follows the order $\mathrm{T} 1>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{Ga}$
(II) Boron has the lowest melting point and boiling point as it is a non-metal
(III) Boron shows a maximum covalency of 4 in its compounds
(IV) The order of atomic radius is $\mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{Al}>$ In
(V) Aluminium is passive to concentrated nitric acid
questions.solution:
Statement (II) is incorrect as Hig has the lowest melting and boiling points instead of $\mathrm{B}$ because of which it is liquid at R.T..
Statemert (IV) is incorrect de correct order of atomic radius is $\mathrm{Ga} < \mathrm{Al} < \mathrm{In}$.
This is due to increasing shells down the group and anomalous behaviour of Ga.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Observe the following statements regarding \(\mathrm{C}_{60}\).
i. All carbons are \(s p^2\)-hybridised.
ii. It contains 12 rings of five carbons each and 20 rings of six carbons each.
iii. It is a non-aromatic compound.
iv. It is pure form of carbon.
v. \(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}\) bond lengths in it are 143.5 and \(138.3 \mathrm{pm}\).
vi. It is prepared by heating graphite in an electric arc in the presence of oxygen.
The correct statements are
questions.solution:
In \(\mathrm{C}_{60}\), all carbon atoms are \(s p^2\)-hybridised. It contains 12 rings of five carbon each and 20 rings of six carbon each. It is anti-aromatic with spherically surrounded by \(\pi\)-electrons. It is a pure form of carbon. \(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}\) bond length in it are 143.5 and \(138.3 \mathrm{pm}\). It is prepared by heating graphite in an electric arc under inert condition (He gas).
Thus, statements (i), (ii), (iv) and (v) are correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Aluminium reacts with $\mathrm{NaOH}$ and forms compound ' $X$ '. If the coordination number of aluminium in ' $X$ ' is 6 , the correct formula of $X$ is
questions.solution:
$2 \mathrm{Al}+2 \mathrm{NaOH}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \underset{\substack{\text { sodium } \\ \text { meta } \\ \text { aluminate }}}{2 \mathrm{NaAlO}_2}+3 \mathrm{H}_2$
Sodium metaaluminate, thus formed, is soluble in water and changes into the complex $\left[\mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_2(\mathrm{OH})_4\right]^{-}$, in which coordination number of $\mathrm{Al}$ is 6 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution: <math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>H</mtext> <mtext>3</mtext> </msub> <msub> <mrow> <mtext>BO</mtext></mrow> <mtext>3</mtext> </msub> <munderover> <mo>→</mo> <mrow> <msub> <mrow> <mtext>-H</mtext></mrow> <mtext>2</mtext> </msub> <mtext>O</mtext></mrow> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mtext>Δ/100</mtext></mrow> <mtext>o</mtext> </msup> <mtext>C</mtext></mrow> </munderover> <msub> <mrow> <mtext>HBO</mtext></mrow> <mtext>2</mtext> </msub> <munderover> <mo>→</mo> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mtext>-160</mtext></mrow> <mtext>o</mtext> </msup> <mtext>C</mtext></mrow> <mtext>Δ</mtext> </munderover> <msub> <mtext>H</mtext> <mtext>4</mtext> </msub> <msub> <mtext>B</mtext> <mtext>2</mtext> </msub> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mtext>7</mtext> </msub> <munderover> <mo>→</mo> <mrow> <mtext>Red</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>Hot </mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>></mtext><mtext> </mtext><msup> <mrow> <mtext>250</mtext></mrow> <mtext>o</mtext> </msup> <mtext> </mtext><mtext>C</mtext></mrow> <mtext>Δ</mtext> </munderover> <msub> <mtext>B</mtext> <mtext>2</mtext> </msub> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mtext>3</mtext> </msub> <mtext> </mtext><msub> <mrow> <mtext>+2H</mtext></mrow> <mtext>2</mtext> </msub> <mtext>O</mtext></mrow></math>

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following elements reacts with steam?
questions.solution:
C, Si and Ge do not react with water while Sn reacts with steam and forms $\mathrm{SnO}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2$.
$\mathrm{Sn}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(g) \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{SnO}_2(g)+2 \mathrm{H}_2(g)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which is an incorrect statement?
options: Diamond is unaffected by conc. acids, but graphite reacts with hot conc. <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> forming mellitic acid <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><msub><mrow><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub></math>
<math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> is toxic because it forms a complex with hemoglobin in the blood

<math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> , carbon suboxide, is a foul-smelling gas

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is called tear gas.
questions.solution: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is phosgene gas and tear gas is phenacyl chloride.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the incorrect statements from the following
I. Tin in +2 state acts as reducing agent while lead in +4 state acts as strong oxidising agent.
II. Silicon exists as both SiF62- and SiCl62- forms.
III. The hybridisation of carbon in fullerene is sp3.
IV. Among Ge, Sn and Pb lowest melting point is for Sn.
questions.solution:
Correct statments are:-
I. Tin in +2 state acts as reducing agent while lead in +4 state acts as strong oxidising agent.
II. Silicon exists as both SiF62- but not as  SiCl62-due to steric hindrance.
III. The hybridisation of carbon in fullerene is sp3.
IV. Among Ge, Sn and Pb lowest melting point is for Pb.

Thus, II and IV are incorrect.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Diborane reacts with ammonia under different conditions to give a variety of products. Which one among the following is not formed in these reactions?
questions.solution:
Diborane reacts with ammonia and gives different products under different reaction conditions as
$\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6+\underset{\text { (excess) }}{\mathrm{NH}_3} \underset{\text { temperature }}{\stackrel{\text { at low }}{\longrightarrow}}$ $\underset{\text {addition product} }{\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6 \cdot 2 \mathrm{NH}_3}$
$\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6+\underset{\text { (excess) }}{\mathrm{NH}_3} \stackrel{\text { high temperature }}{\longrightarrow}$ $\underset{\text {boron nitride (resembles with graphite) }}{(\mathrm{BN})_n}$
$\underset{1}{\mathrm{~B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6}\underset{:}{+}\underset{2}{\mathrm{NH}_3 }\stackrel{\text { high temperature }}{\longrightarrow}$ $\underset{\text {borazine (inorganic benzene)}}{\mathrm{B}_3 \mathrm{~N}_3 \mathrm{H}_6}$
Thus, $\mathrm{B}_{12} \mathrm{H}_{12}$ is not the product of reaction between diborane and ammonia.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The building units of various crystalline forms of boron are:
questions.solution: Crystalline boron consists of B12 icosahedral units having 12 corners and twenty faces. Each corner is represented by a thickened circle as shown in the figure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is used in the estimation of carbon monoxide?
questions.solution:
Carbon monoxide is estimated using $\mathrm{I}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ as per the following reaction:
$$
\mathrm{I}_2 \mathrm{O}_5+5 \mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow \underset{\text { violet }}{\mathrm{I}_2}+5 \mathrm{CO}_2
$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: The reaction between boron and concentrated $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ gives $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}$
Amorphous boron on heating with oxygen forms $\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$
Boron is a non-conductor of electricity
questions.solution:
Boron reacts with concentrated $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ to give orthoboric acid and nitrogen dioxide.
$\mathrm{B}+3 \mathrm{HNO}_3 \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{BO}_3+3 \mathrm{NO}_2$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Select the incorrect statement.
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> can be stored in a vessel made of aluminium

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> can be stored in a vessel made of Be/Aluminium alloy
<math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> can be stored in a vessel coated of wax
questions.solution:
2Al+2NaOH+2H2O→NaAlO2+3H2
Aluminium dis Solutionves in aqueous alkali, thus NaOH cannot be stored in a vessel made of aluminium

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> is used as a reducing agent
questions.solution: For Tl , due to inert pair effect, +3 oxidation state is less stable and Tl3+ gets reduced to Tl1+. So it does not get disproportionated, rather get reduced.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Boron reacts with air to give
questions.solution:
Boron is a chemical element with the symbol B and atomic number 5. In its crystalline form it is a brittle, dark, lustrous metalloid; in its amorphous form it is a brown powder.

Air contains O2, N2 in max proportions
4B+3O2→2B2O32B+N2→2BN

Boron reacts with air to give the mixture of B2O3 and BN.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following statements:
I. In diamond, each carbon atom is $s p^3$-hybridised.
II. Graphite has planar hexagonal layers of carbon atoms.
III. Silicones being surrounded by non-polar alkyl groups are water repelling in nature
IV. The order of catenation in group 14 elements is $\mathrm{Si}>\mathrm{C}>\mathrm{Sn}>\mathrm{Ge}>\mathrm{Pb}$.

The correct statements are
questions.solution:
(I, II) In diamond, hybridisation of carbon is $s p^3$ and graphite has planar hexagonal layer of $\mathrm{C}$-atoms. The hybridisation of each $\mathrm{C}$-atom in graphite is $s p^2$.
(III) Silicones are organo-silicon polymers formed from inorganic and organic monomer having chemical formula $\left(R_2 \mathrm{SiO}\right)_n$, due to non-polar alkyl group, silicones are water repelling in nature.
(IV) The catenation tendency in group 14 elements decreases due to decrease in element bond strength. Thus, the correct order is
$$
\mathrm{C}>>\mathrm{Si}>\mathrm{Ge} \approx \mathrm{Sn}
$$

Hence, statement (IV) is incorrect.
Thus, option (a) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the correct statements from the following :
I. $\mathrm{Ga}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ is an amphoteric oxide.
II. The dimer of aluminium chloride has three $\mathrm{Al}-\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{Al}$ bridge bonds.
III. Boron is very hard refractory solid of high melting temperature.
questions.solution:
(I) $\mathrm{Ga}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ is an amphoteric oxide is the correct statement as it reacts with acid and base both, as shown below : Reaction with an acid $$ \mathrm{Ga}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+6 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{GaCl}_3+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} $$ Reaction with a base $$ \mathrm{Ga}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+2 \mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaGaO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} $$ (II) The dimer of aluminium chloride has three $\mathrm{Al}-\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{Al}$ bridge bond is not the correct statement as structure of $\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_6$ is as follows:
(III) Boron is very hard refractory solid of high melting temperature is the correct statement as its melting point is $2349 \mathrm{~K}$ and is used as refractory solid. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following metal halide is more covalent?
questions.solution:
Key Idea Fajan’s rules help in predicting the covalent character in bond.
SbCl5 metal halide is more covalent than other given options. It can be explained on the basis of Fajan’s rule. Higher the charge on the cation, greater is its polarising power. In SbCl5, Antimony (Sb) is in + 5 oxidation state. It has maximum polarising power. Hence, possess higher covalent character.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Which of the following option is correct regarding the structure of borax?
options: Two triangular and two tetrahedral units
Three triangular and one tetrahedral units
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following methods is used to prepare carbon monoxide on commercial scale?
options: Dehydration of formic acid with conc. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
Direct oxidation of C in limited supply of oxygen
questions.solution:
(a) When formic acid $\mathrm{HCOOH}$ is dehydrated with $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ (conc.), it gives carbon monoxide (CO) and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ as :
$$
\mathrm{HCOOH}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \text { (conc.) } \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4
$$

But the above process is not used on commercial scale.
(b) Direct oxidation of carbon in limited supply of oxygen can give $\mathrm{CO}$ but the process is not used on commercial scale.
$$
2 \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{O}_2 \text { (limited supply) } \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{CO}
$$
(c) When steam $\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)$ is passed over coke $(\mathrm{C})$, it gives $\mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{H}_2$, as :
$$
\text { Coke }(\mathrm{C})+\underset{(\text { Steam) }}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}} \stackrel{\Delta}{\mathrm{Ni}} \mathrm{CO}(g)+\mathrm{H}_2(g)
$$

The above process can be used to produce (CO) on commercial scale.
(d) Lime stone is $\mathrm{CaCO}_3$, on heating it gives oxygen $\left(\mathrm{O}_2\right)$ and $\mathrm{CaO}$ as :
$$
\mathrm{CaCO}_3 \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{CaO}+\mathrm{O}_2
$$

Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The following statements are made in connection with carbon dioxide CO2.
I. Carbon dioxide is prepared by burning carbon or by the reaction of a carbonate and an acid or by decomposition of lime stone.
II. In all these methods of preparation of CO2, the elements carbon and oxygen are available in a fixed ratio 3: 8.
III. When CO2 is continuously bubbled through lime water, initially lime water turns milky and then again colourless.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
I. Carbon dioxide is prepared by burning carbon (or) by the reaction of a carbonate and an acid (or) by decomposition of lime stone.

CaCO3  + 2HCl  →CaCl2  +H2O  + CO2

CaCO3→∆CaO  +CO2

II. Samples of carbon dioxide contain carbon and oxygen in the mass ratio of 3 : 8

C  123+O2328 → CO2

III.  When carbon dioxide is continuously bubbled through lime water, initially due to the formation of insoluble calcium carbonate lime water turns milky and then again when reacted with excess carbon dioxide due to soluble calcium bicarbonate it becomes colourless.

Ca(OH)2  +CO2 →CaCO3white precipitateCaCO3+ H2O+  CO2excess  ​⟶   Ca(HCO3)2water soluble ​   ​
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
What are x and y in the following reaction?

x Pb3O4→yPbO+O2
questions.solution:
Lead(II,IV) oxide, also called red lead or minium or Lead tetroxide , is the inorganic compound with the formula Pb3O4. A bright red or orange solid, it is used as pigment, in the manufacture of batteries, and rustproof primer paints. It is an example of a mixed valence compound

Pb3O4 consists of PbO and PbO2

Oxidation state of Pb in PbO is +2

and in PbO2 is +4

When heated to 500 °C, it decomposes to lead(II) oxide and oxygen. At 580 °C, the reaction is complete

2Pb3O4→6PbO+O2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The gas produced by the passage of air over hot coke is
questions.solution:
The gas produced by passage of air over hot coke is called producer gas. The reaction proceeds as:
$$
2 \mathrm{C}+\underbrace{\mathrm{O}_2+\mathrm{N}_2}_{\text {Coke }} \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} \underbrace{2 \mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{N}_2}_{\text {Producer gas }}
$$
Other carbonaceous (carbon containing) fuels also produce producer gas of different compositions.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ in water at $\mathrm{pH} < 7$ forms
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ form $\left[\mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+}$ in water due to acidic medium $(\mathrm{pH} < 7)$.
$$
\mathrm{AlCl}_3+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow\left[\mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+}+3 \mathrm{Cl}^{\ominus}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Among the following oxides, which one is weakly basic and yet a powerful oxidant?
questions.solution:
For the dioxides of group- 14 elements \(\left(\mathrm{CO}_2, \mathrm{SiO}_2, \mathrm{GeO}_2\right.\) and \(\left.\mathrm{PbO}_2\right), \mathrm{PbO}_2\) is most basic in nature. Again, in oxidation state \(\mathrm{Pb}\) in +4 , which is less \(\mathrm{PbO}_2\) stable than its +2 state because of inert pair effect.
So, \(\mathrm{Pb}^{4+}\) of \(\mathrm{PbO}_2\) has a tendency to be reduced into more stable \(\mathrm{Pb}^{2+}\) (PbO) showing strong oxidising nature of \(\mathrm{PbO}_2\).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is water-gas shift reaction?
questions.solution:
The mixture of CO  and H2 is called as water gas. As the mixture of CO and H2 is used for the synthesis of methanol and a number of hydrocarbons, it is also called 'syn gas' or synthesis gas.

The process of producing 'syn gas' from coal is called coal gasification.

Cs + H2Og → COg + H2g

The production of dihydrogen can be increased by reacting carbon monoxide with steam in the presence of iron chromate as a catalyst.

COg + H2Og → CO2g + H2g

This is called water gas shift reaction.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
An element 'E' gives following reaction sequence :

E+N2→High temp.EN→High temp.waterE(OH)3+NH3

Element E may be:
questions.solution:
Aluminium and boron forms nitride with nitrogen at higher temperature due to the high bond energy of nitrogen.

The reaction of boron and nitrogen forms boron nitride which is then hydrolysed to form products as follows:

2B+N2→High temp.2BN→High temp.waterB(OH)3+NH3

Aluminium forms aluminium nitride and it further hydrolysed as follows:

2Al+N2→High temp.2AlN→High temp.waterAl(OH)3+NH3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the correct statement(s) from the following:
I. The catenation property of group 14 elements decreases form carbon to tin.
II. Fullerene $\left(\mathrm{C}_{60}\right)$ has 20 five-membered carbon rings and 12 six-membered carbon rings.
III. $\mathrm{SiO}_2$ is soluble in conc. $\mathrm{NaOH}$.
questions.solution:
(I) Catenation property of group 14 elements decreases from carbon to tin. Carbon is small atom, thus the bond formed between two carbon atom is strong. Therefore, it shows maximum catenation. As we move down the group, size of atom increases, so $M-M$ bond strength decreases and tendency to show catenation decreases. Thus, lead does not show catenation property.
(II) Fullerene $\left(\mathrm{C}_{60}\right)$ has 20 six-membered carbon rings and 12 five-membered carbon rings.
(III) $\mathrm{SiO}_2$ is soluble in $\mathrm{NaOH}$ or alkaline solution. $\mathrm{SiO}_2$ is an acidic oxide.
$$
2 \mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{SiO}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SiO}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}
$$

Thus, option (2) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Gas A is bubbled through slaked lime when a white precipitate is formed. On prolonged bubbling the precipitate is dissolved. On heating the resultant solution, the white precipitate reappears with evolution of gas B. The gases A and B respectively are
questions.solution:
CO 2 + Ca OH 2 ⟶ CaCO 3 ↓ + H 2 O A white ppt

CaCO 3 + CO 2 + H 2 O ⟶ Ca HCO 3 2 Calcium bicarbonate (Souble in water)

Ca HCO 3 2 ⟶ Heat CaCO 3 + CO 2 + H 2 O B

A and B are CO2 and CO2 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Match the following
questions.solution:
Carbon black is used as a filler in automobile tyres due to its amorphous nature.
Graphite is used as electrodes in batteries due to being an electrical conductor.
Diamond is used as an abrasive due to its hard nature. Activated charcoal is used in air conditioning systems due to absorbing nature.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An element $(X)$ when burnt in oxygen forms neutral $X \mathrm{O}$ and acidic $X \mathrm{O}_2$. The element $X$ is
questions.solution: $\mathrm{C}$, when burnt in oxygen forms neutral $\mathrm{CO}$ and acidic $\mathrm{CO}_2$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the incorrect statements from the following.

I. Tin in +2 state acts as reducing agent while lead in +4 state acts as strong oxidising agent.
II. Silicon exists as both $\left[\mathrm{SiF}_6\right]^{2-}$ and $\left[\mathrm{SiCl}_6\right]^{2-}$ forms.
III. The hybridisation of carbon in fullerene is $s p^3$.
IV. Among $\mathrm{Ge}, \mathrm{Sn}$ and $\mathrm{Pb}$ lowest melting point is for $\mathrm{Sn}$.
questions.solution:
(II). $\left[\mathrm{SiF}_6\right]^{2-}$ is known but $\left[\mathrm{SiCl}_6\right]^{2-}$ does not exist, this is because due to larger size of $\mathrm{Cl}$ atom as compared to $\mathrm{F}$ atom it is not possible to accommodate six atoms of $\mathrm{Cl}$ around $\mathrm{Si}$ atom. (III). Fullerens structure have carbon atoms both in $s p^2$ and $s p^3$ hybridised system.
Correct option is (c).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The stability of +1 oxidation state increases in the sequence
questions.solution:
Group - 13 elements exhibit +3 and +1 oxidation states.  Stability of the lower oxidation state increases on moving down the group due to inert pair effect.
Al+ < Ga+ < In+ < Tl+
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Aluminium chloride exists as dimmer, <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub></math> in solid state as well as in solution: of non–polar solvents such as benzene. when dissolved in water, it gives
options: <math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><msub><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math>
questions.solution:
AlCl3 is covalent but in water, it become ionic due to large hydration energy it dissociated as follows
AlCl3+6H2O⇌AlH2O63++3Cl-

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The correct statements among I to III regrading group 13 element oxides are,
(I) Boron trioxide is acidic.
(II) Oxides of aluminum and gallium are amphoteric.
(III) Oxides of indium and thallium are basic.
questions.solution:
All statements are correct
B2O3→ acidic , B2O3 is an anhydride of Orthoboric Acid.
Al2O3 and Ga2O3 are amphoteric, amphoteric oxides can react with acids as well as bases.
oxides of ln and Tl are basic.
As we move down the group basicity of oxides increases due to increase in metallic character of elements
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
